## Summary

Security: Protocol-relative third-party stylesheet URLs allow insecure dependency loading

## Problem

**Severity**: `Medium` | **File**: `apps/realworld/index.html:L6`

The page loads external CSS via protocol-relative URLs (`//...`). If the page is ever served over HTTP (or behind a misconfigured proxy), these dependencies can be fetched insecurely and altered by a man-in-the-middle, enabling content/script injection through CSS or compromised assets.

## Solution

Use explicit `https://` URLs for all third-party resources, enforce HSTS on the hosting domain, and avoid protocol-relative URLs.

## Changes

- `apps/realworld/index.html` (modified)

### `@neo-opus-4-7` commented on 2026-05-17T21:42:41Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ Hi @tomaioo — late response on this, sorry for the wait. Quick context that explains why this isn't a straightforward merge:
> 
> ### Substrate History
> 
> Neo released GA in November 2019. `apps/realworld` was Neo's **2nd ever app**, built specifically for the [RealWorld framework comparison project](https://github.com/realworld-apps/realworld) (formerly known as gothinkster/realworld) — a comparison set of 100+ JS framework/library implementations of the same "Conduit" social-blog spec.
> 
> ### Conformance Constraint
> 
> RealWorld's acceptance criteria for entries were strict: even slight template mismatches would exclude an implementation from the comparison set. This is why Neo's `apps/realworld` couldn't use Neo's own component library — the canonical `index.html` template is the entry contract.
> 
> ### The Protocol-Relative URLs Are the Spec
> 
> The exact pattern in `apps/realworld/index.html:L6` appears verbatim in the [RealWorld frontend template specification](https://github.com/realworld-apps/realworld/blob/main/docs/src/content/docs/specifications/frontend/templates.md):
> 
> ```html
> <link href="//code.ionicframework.com/ionicons/2.0.1/css/ionicons.min.css" ... />
> <link href="//fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=..." ... />
> <link rel="stylesheet" href="//demo.productionready.io/main.css" />
> ```
> 
> Changing Neo's implementation diverges from the canonical template and could lose Neo's entry in the comparison set.
> 
> ### Path Forward
> 
> The substrate-correct way to land this is **upstream first**:
> 
> 1. PR the change to [realworld-apps/realworld](https://github.com/realworld-apps/realworld) (the canonical template spec)
> 2. If accepted, the 100+ downstream implementations (Neo's included) can all update to match
> 3. We'll gladly merge this PR once the upstream change lands
> 
> The upstream path is non-trivial because of the 100+ apps cascade — the upstream maintainers may decide the protocol-relative pattern is intentional (it predates universal HSTS, but it's a documented design choice in the spec rather than an oversight). That dilemma is real.
> 
> The technical concern you raised is valid in production HTTP contexts. The constraint here is purely the comparison-conformance contract that defines what `apps/realworld` is — diverging from the canonical template breaks the demo's whole purpose.
> 
> Holding this PR open as `awaiting-upstream` rather than closing — if you do file the upstream PR and it lands, ping us and we'll merge promptly.
> 
> — @neo-opus-4-7 (Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Code) on behalf of Neo maintainers

---

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Approved

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Approve
- **Rationale**: This is a narrow external security hygiene fix. The diff changes only `apps/realworld/index.html` and replaces protocol-relative third-party stylesheet URLs with explicit `https://` URLs. Fresh validation found no residual protocol-relative `href` / `src` / `url(...)` entries in the touched file.

**Peer-Review Opening:** Thanks for the focused security cleanup. The patch is small, directly addresses the insecure dependency-loading vector described in the PR, and does not disturb the RealWorld app boot path.
